vocabulary.name: Weather Underground PWS API Vocabulary
vocabulary.description: Terminology and domain concepts used in the Weather Underground Personal Weather Station API.

terms.label: Personal Weather Station
terms.definition: A privately owned, individually operated weather monitoring device that uploads data to the Weather Underground network. PWS owners contribute hyperlocal weather data in exchange for free API access.

terms.label: Station Identifier
terms.definition: A unique alphanumeric code assigned to each PWS upon registration with the Weather Underground network. Format is typically a country/state prefix followed by a city abbreviation and numeric suffix (e.g., KCASARAT1).

terms.label: Unix Epoch Timestamp
terms.definition: The number of seconds elapsed since 1970-01-01T00:00:00Z (UTC). Used to represent observation timestamps in a timezone-independent format.

terms.label: Observation Time UTC
terms.definition: The date and time at which the observation was recorded, expressed in Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) using ISO 8601 format.

terms.label: Observation Time Local
terms.definition: The date and time at which the observation was recorded, expressed in the local time zone of the weather station.

terms.label: Quality Control Status
terms.definition: A flag indicating whether the observation has passed automated quality control checks. A value of 1 indicates the data passed QC; values of -1 or 0 may indicate suspect or unverified data.

terms.label: Temperature
terms.definition: The current air temperature measured at the station, expressed in the unit system specified by the request (Fahrenheit for imperial, Celsius for metric and SI).

terms.label: Dew Point
terms.definition: The temperature at which the air must be cooled (at constant pressure and humidity) for condensation to occur. A measure of atmospheric moisture content.

terms.label: Heat Index
terms.definition: An index combining air temperature and relative humidity to produce an apparent temperature (what it 'feels like'). Relevant when temperatures exceed 80°F/27°C.

terms.label: Wind Chill
terms.definition: The perceived decrease in air temperature due to the flow of air. Calculated from actual temperature and wind speed; relevant in cold conditions.

terms.label: Wind Direction
terms.definition: The direction from which the wind is blowing, expressed in meteorological degrees (0° = North, 90° = East, 180° = South, 270° = West).

terms.label: Wind Direction Cardinal
terms.definition: The wind direction expressed as a compass cardinal or intercardinal point abbreviation (N, NE, E, SE, S, SW, W, NW, and 8 additional intermediate points).

terms.label: Wind Speed
terms.definition: The average speed of the wind over the observation interval.

terms.label: Wind Gust
terms.definition: The peak instantaneous wind speed measured during the observation interval, typically exceeding the average wind speed by a significant margin.

terms.label: Atmospheric Pressure
terms.definition: The force per unit area exerted by the atmosphere. Reported as station pressure, which has not been corrected for altitude.

terms.label: Relative Humidity
terms.definition: The ratio of the current water vapor content in the air to the maximum possible water vapor content at the same temperature, expressed as a percentage (0–100%).

terms.label: Precipitation Rate
terms.definition: The current intensity of precipitation expressed as an equivalent rainfall depth per hour. A value of 0 indicates no precipitation.

terms.label: Precipitation Total
terms.definition: The total accumulated precipitation since midnight local time (or since the last bucket tip reset). Resets each day at midnight.

terms.label: Solar Radiation
terms.definition: The intensity of solar (shortwave) radiation reaching the surface, measured in watts per square meter (W/m²). Requires a dedicated solar radiation sensor.

terms.label: UV Index
terms.definition: The World Health Organization (WHO) standard measurement of the ultraviolet radiation level from the sun at a particular place and time. Scale ranges from 0 (low) to 11+ (extreme). Requires a UV sensor.

terms.label: Elevation
terms.definition: The height of the weather station above mean sea level. Used for pressure corrections and altitude-related calculations.

terms.label: Unit System
terms.definition: The measurement system for numerical weather values. Valid options: 'e' (Imperial/English: °F, mph, inHg, in), 'm' (Metric: °C, km/h, mbar, mm), 's' (SI: °C, km/h, hPa, mm), 'h' (UK Hybrid: °C, mph, mbar, mm).

terms.label: Numeric Precision
terms.definition: Optional request parameter. When set to 'decimal', numeric values are returned with decimal precision rather than rounded integers. Recommended for sensor data analysis.

terms.label: Geographic Coordinate
terms.definition: A comma-separated latitude and longitude pair used to specify a geographic location for forecast or location-search queries (e.g., '37.774,-122.419').

terms.label: Daily Summary
terms.definition: An aggregated record representing all observations from a single calendar day, including minimum, maximum, and average values for each measured quantity.

terms.label: Hyperlocal Weather Data
terms.definition: Weather observations collected at a very precise geographic location, often within a residential area. Contrasted with official NWS/airport station data which may be miles away. PWS data is hyperlocal by definition.
